DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-21-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JUDITH ANDERSON'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: William E. Shapiro, Esq., Law Office of William Shapiro, Esq. Law Offices of William Shapiro (415) 678-9209, appearing for Plaintiff. There are no other appearances. Counsel is appearing to have Order signed. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer to answer sustained with ten days leave to amend. Defendants must clarify their defenses with respect to the specific causes of action and supply facts supporting defenses that require facts, such as waiver, estoppel, unclean hands, laches, etc. Order signed in open Court.(302CWW/AN)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

Notice Of Deft'S Motion And Motion To Compel Pltf To Provide Verifications And For Sanction
Off Calendar Mar-18-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Binafar S. Jaigirdar, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Philip V. Lyons
Mary Catherine Wiederhold, Esq., for Plaintiff and opposing party: Judith Anderson
After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted.
2. Defendant Philip Lyon's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Verifications And For Sanctions is granted.
a. Plaintiff shall provide verifications to discovery made of the responding party's personal knowledge, and not on information or belief;
b. Request for sanctions denied. Order signed in open court.
Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deft Phillip Lyons'S Answers To Pltf'S Lorm Interrogatories, Set One, Request For Monetary Sanctions,
Off Calendar Mar-18-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Mary Catherine Wiederhold, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Judith Anderson
Binafar S. Jaigirdar, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Philip V. Lyons
After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted.
1. Plaintiff Judith Anderson's Motion To Compel Defendant Philip Lyon's Answers To Form Interrogatories [Set One], Request For Monetary Sanctions is granted in part, denied in part:
a. Request for supplemental response to Form Interrogatory No. 2.8, moot, based on recent supplemental response
b. Request for supplemental response to Form Interrogatory Nos. 7.2, 7.3, 16.2, and 16.3.denied;
c. Request for sanctions denied.
Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Document Requests And Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf And Her Counsel
Off Calendar Mar-18-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Jessica Pliner, Esq., for Defendant and moving party: Better Property Management
Mary Catherine Wiederhold, Esq., for Plaintiff and opposing party: Judith Anderson
After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted as modified.
Defendant Better Property Management's Motion To Compel Responses To Document Requests, And Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, And Request For Monetary Sanctions, granted in part, denied in part:
a. Motion To Compel Responses To Document Requests granted:
i. all objections are waived except attorney- client and attorney work product privileges;
ii. plaintiff shall provide a full, complete and verified responses to defendant Better Property Management's Request for Production of Documents within five Court days of the date of service of this order;
b. Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories granted:
i. all objections are waived except attorney- client and attorney work product privileges;
ii. Within five court days of this hearing, the parties shall meet and confer to agree on a case specific definition of the term "incident" to be applied to the subject interrogatories;
iii. Thereafter, and within five court days of the meet and confer agreement, plaintiff shall provide full, complete and verified supplemental responses to defendant's form interrogatories.
iv. If parties cannot agree, they may request a telephonic conference with the Court for guidence.
c. Request For Monetary Sanctions granted; sanctions shall issue in the sum of $540.00, to run against plaintiff and counsel. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.
